evals.expected_output: A consult file written to ~/dev/ai/skills/peer-review/reviews/{session}-consult.md with: (1) properly expanded ISO timestamp (not a literal shell expression), (2) agent field set to 'Claude Code' not a model name, (3) meaningful body describing the JWT code and what to look for, (4) NO focus field in YAML since no specific question was given, (5) user told where the file is and what to expect next

A consult file at ~/dev/ai/skills/peer-review/reviews/rate-limit-review-consult.md with: (1) session name exactly 'rate-limit-review' (user-specified), (2) focus field in YAML set to the sliding window question, (3) properly expanded timestamp, (4) body contains the code and the specific review question, (5) tmux pane discovery step attempted or user prompted for target

Skill lists existing .md files in ~/dev/ai/skills/peer-review/reviews/, groups them by session prefix, asks the user which ones to remove, and deletes only those confirmed. Uses exact full paths, no wildcards, no rm -rf.
